title: Tests should not execute any code after done() is called
description: The done callback of Mocha is used to signal the end of an asynchronous test. It is called when the test is complete, either successfully or with an error. It is important not to follow the done callback with code because the test may not have completed yet, and the code may execute before the test is finished.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Do not execute code after done() is called in tests
